input.prompt: List ALL settings, menus, and options available in the RustDesk mobile app. Include display settings, codec, quality, FPS, gestures, touch mode, toolbar options, and any session settings. Describe exactly what users see in the mobile interface.

content.content:
# RustDesk Android App Settings & Options

Based on the documentation provided, here are the main settings and interface elements available in the RustDesk Android mobile app:

## Control Modes
The app offers two input control modes accessible via the lower toolbar:
- **Mouse mode**: Includes right-click functionality via two-finger tap
- **Touch mode**: Direct touch interaction with remote device

## Navigation & Primary Functions
The bottom navigation bar provides access to:
- Device home page (history and connections)
- Settings menu
- Share Screen functionality

## Settings Menu
Under the Settings section, users can configure:
- **ID/Relay Server**: Customize server hostname/IP, relay server, API server, and encryption key
- QR code scanning option for quick server configuration

## Screen Sharing & Permissions
The Share Screen interface allows toggling of:
- Screen Capture permission
- Input Control (requires system accessibility settings)
- File Transfer permission
- Audio Capture (requires Android 10+)

## File Transfer Interface
The file manager displays:
- Home directory navigation
- Breadcrumb directory path
- Multi-select capability (long-press activation)
- Local/Remote page switching
- Absolute path and folder statistics

The documentation focuses primarily on these core features rather than detailed display settings like codec, quality, or FPS parameters.

if [[ -f package.json && $(git status --porcelain | grep -E '\.js$|\.jsx$|\.ts$|\.tsx$') ]]; then echo 'Analyzing bundle size impact...'; if command -v bundlesize >/dev/null 2>&1; then npx bundlesize; elif npm list webpack-bundle-analyzer >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'Use: npx webpack-bundle-analyzer build/static/js/*.js'; else echo 'No bundle analysis tool found'; fi; fi
